facts.text: Key West’s historic Porter House on Caroline Street and Gato House on Virginia Street are listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Duval Street, the island’s main thoroughfare, is named after Florida’s first territorial governor, William Pope Duval.
Pan American Airlines began in Key West in 1926, initially ferrying passengers to Havana, Cuba.
José Martí visited Key West multiple times starting in 1891 to recruit Cuban independence fighters and founded the Cuban Revolutionary Party there.
The Great Fire of Key West on April 1, 1886, destroyed 18 cigar factories and 614 houses after starting in a coffee shop beside the San Carlos Institute.
